questions.text: If a < b and c < 0, then:
questions.explanation: Multiplying or dividing an inequality by a negative number reverses the sign.

questions.text: The solution set of 3x - 5 < 7 for x ∈ N is:
questions.explanation: 3x < 12 => x < 4. Natural numbers less than 4 are {1, 2, 3}.

questions.text: Which of the following is a strict inequality?
questions.explanation: Strict inequalities use < or > signs, not ≤ or ≥.

options: Right of and including line x=3
questions.explanation: x ≥ 3 represents all points where the x-coordinate is 3 or more.
-2x ≤ 6 => x ≥ -3 (reversing the sign when dividing by -2).
Modulus inequality |x| < a means x lies between -a and a.

questions.text: For x ∈ R, the interval notation for x > 2 is:
questions.explanation: Parentheses ( ) are used when the endpoint is not included.

questions.text: A line is dotted in a graph if the inequality is:
questions.explanation: Dotted lines indicate that points on the boundary are not part of the solution.

questions.text: The number of integers satisfying -3 < x ≤ 2 is:
questions.explanation: Integers are -2, -1, 0, 1, 2. Total 5.
Multiplying by -1 reverses the inequality.

questions.text: Represent x ≤ 5 on a number line:
questions.explanation: Less than or equal to means a closed point and values to the left.

questions.text: The region x > 0, y > 0 is quadrant:
questions.explanation: Both coordinates are positive in the first quadrant.

questions.text: Solution set of x/3 > x/2 + 1 is:

questions.explanation: Bracket [ includes, parenthesis ) excludes.

questions.text: The inequality y < 2 represents the region:
questions.explanation: y values are less than 2 below the horizontal line.

questions.text: To solve system of inequalities, we find the ________ region.
questions.explanation: The solution must satisfy all inequalities simultaneously.
2x > 6 => x > 3. Next integer is 4.

questions.text: If x is a real number and x² < 9, then:
questions.explanation: x must lie between the roots -3 and 3.

questions.text: Division of an inequality by a positive number changes the sign.
questions.explanation: Positive division preserves the inequality direction.

questions.text: Graphical solution of x + y ≤ 5 includes origin because:
questions.explanation: We test the origin (0,0) to see if it satisfies the inequality.
The reciprocal is negative only if the number itself is negative.
Length = Upper bound - Lower bound = 10 - 2 = 8.

questions.text: If a/c > b/c and c > 0, then:
questions.explanation: Since c is positive, multiplication by c preserves the sign.
This interval covers the entire number line.
x is either more than 2 or less than -2.
2x < 6 => x < 3. Natural numbers are {1, 2}.
Product or quotient is positive if both numbers are positive or both are negative.
Multiplying by -3 reverses the inequality.

questions.text: The set {x : x ∈ R, x ≤ a} is:
questions.explanation: Standard set-builder to interval conversion.

questions.text: Linear inequalities in two variables represent a:
questions.explanation: The solution is a region on one side of the line.
Strict inequality does not include the boundary value.

questions.text: Number of natural numbers satisfying x + 3 ≤ 3 is:
questions.explanation: x ≤ 0. There are no natural numbers ≤ 0 (N starts from 1).

questions.text: If a > b and b > c, then a > c. This is ________ property.
questions.explanation: Property of ordering.
Multiply by -1: 0 > x > -5, which is -5 < x < 0.
